title: MAINTAINERS.md: sort by role and add nalind to OWNERS
body:
MAINTAINERS.md: sort by role 

This should make it easier to read, most people likely care about the
roles.

---

OWNERS: add nalind

He is a core maintainer and was not listed here by mistake.

---

.github/labeler: add governance label automatically

We don't have anything CI wise to enforce proper reviews for governance
changes but we can easily label a PR so that should raise more
awareness.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: (Overall I would assume basic automation would be nice to make sure the files are sorted correctly and that MAINTAINERS and OWNERS files are always in sync)
Add to that: We should add some form of label on edit of this files to trigger additional scrutiny.
Also: we should not merge this without a majority of core maintainers LGTMing

> Add to that: We should add some form of label on edit of this files to trigger additional scrutiny.

Did you look at the third commit? That is what I have added via governance label here in the last commit! I think it only works post merge though.

LGTM

I personally dont think we need a majority on this item given there is no substantive change to governance.  Any change in persons or role, sure.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mtrmac @nalind One of you mind dropping an LGTM?

@mheon So how should we count the majority vote?
4 out of 6 LGTM from core maintainers? I mean I did a the PR so my LGTM should be implied and then I got three others so we are at 4 already per my assumption. If my vote wouldn't count just because I did the PR it would be kinda pointless?

Yeah, you're right, I think we're good to merge given this.
/lgtm
